input: How many API keys can I have per project on the Starter plan?
expected_output: Up to 5 API keys per project on the Starter plan.

expected_contexts.text: Starter plan: Up to 5 API keys per project. 10,000 requests per month included. Community support only.

rationale: The pricing doc states the Starter plan allows up to 5 API keys per project; the answer must match that exact number.

input: How many requests per month does the Pro plan include?
expected_output: The Pro plan includes 1,000,000 requests per month.

expected_contexts.text: Pro plan: Up to 50 API keys per project. 1,000,000 requests per month included. Email support with a 24-hour response time.

rationale: The Pro section explicitly lists 1,000,000 requests/month; the figure must come from the doc, not be estimated.

expected_output: All plans store data in the US (us-east-1).

expected_contexts.text: Data residency: All plans store data in the US (us-east-1). EU data residency is available on the Enterprise plan only.

rationale: The residency section states all plans store data in the US (us-east-1); EU residency is Enterprise-only and was not asked.

input: What is the support response time on the Pro plan?
expected_output: Pro includes email support with a 24-hour response time.

expected_contexts.text: Pro plan: Up to 50 API keys per project. 1,000,000 requests per month included. Email support with a 24-hour response time.

rationale: The Pro section specifies email support with a 24-hour response time; the answer must cite that SLA.

input: How many requests are included each month on the Starter plan?
expected_output: The Starter plan includes 10,000 requests per month.

expected_contexts.text: Starter plan: Up to 5 API keys per project. 10,000 requests per month included. Community support only.

rationale: The Starter section lists 10,000 requests/month included; this is distinct from the Pro figure and must not be confused with it.

input: Does the Starter plan include SCIM SSO provisioning?
expected_output: I don't have that information; the provided context doesn't mention SCIM or SSO for the Starter plan.

expected_contexts.text: Starter plan: Up to 5 API keys per project. 10,000 requests per month included. Community support only.

rationale: The context covers keys/requests/support for Starter but says nothing about SCIM or SSO; the correct behavior is to decline, not invent a capability.

input: Is there 24/7 phone support on any plan?
expected_output: I don't have that information; the context only mentions community and email support, not phone support.

expected_contexts.text: Starter: Community support only. Pro: Email support with a 24-hour response time.

rationale: The context mentions only community and email support; there is no phone-support information, so the agent must decline rather than guess.

input: What is the price in euros for the Pro plan?
expected_output: I don't have that information; the provided context doesn't list prices in euros.

expected_contexts.text: Pro plan: Up to 50 API keys per project. 1,000,000 requests per month included. Email support with a 24-hour response time.

rationale: The doc contains no pricing in euros (or any currency figures); the agent must decline rather than fabricate a price.
